name: apt-listchanges
title: apt-listchanges
description:
package change history notification tool
The tool apt-listchanges can compare a new version of a
package with the one currently installed and show what has been
changed, by extracting the relevant entries from the Debian changelog
and NEWS files.
.
It can be run on several .deb archives at a time to get a list of all
changes that would be caused by installing or upgrading a group of
packages. When configured as an APT plugin it will do this
automatically during upgrades.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use apt-listchanges because package change history notification tool.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use apt-listchanges as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `apt-listchanges [[options...]] {[--apt] | [package.deb...]}` as documented upstream for apt-listchanges.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair apt-listchanges with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `apt-listchanges --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: apt-listchanges - Show new changelog entries from Debian package archives
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install apt-listchanges. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `apt-listchanges [[options...]] {[--apt] | [package.deb...]}`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: package change history notification tool.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: apt-listchanges - Show new changelog entries from Debian package archives.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install apt-listchanges
Install with: sudo apt install apt-listchanges
